title.zh: 太陽能變戰爭武器？以色列與巴勒斯坦的能源安全角力
title.en: Solar Power as a Shield: How Energy Became a Weapon in Israel-Palestine Conflict

summary.zh:
處於長期衝突的以色列與巴勒斯坦，竟讓太陽能成為戰略武器？通常而言，領土爭端會阻礙再生能源發展，但這場衝突卻反其道而行之。研究指出，以色列、巴勒斯坦權力機構與哈馬斯，皆將太陽能視為維護國家安全的關鍵工具，甚至不惜犧牲經濟效益與環境考量。這種「能源安全」的思維，不僅改變了太陽能的應用場景，更重塑了雙方在爭議地區的戰略布局。

具體而言，太陽能項目主要服務三大安全目標：其一，分散發電以避免加薩走廊遭空襲或以色列遭火箭攻擊時電網癱瘓；其二，在東耶路撒冷等分裂城市，透過屋頂太陽能板實現離網供電，防止共用電網遭政治操弄；其三，於約旦河西岸的B區與C區 strategically 佈置太陽能基地，確保自身能源供應並阻擋對手擴張。這些舉措顯示，太陽能已從純粹的綠色能源，轉化為戰略博弈的載體。

研究進一步探討，為何此衝突特別適合將太陽能納入國安架構。相較於其他領土爭端，以色列與巴勒斯坦的高風險環境、碎片化的治理結構，以及雙方對能源自主的迫切需求，共同促成太陽能的軍事化轉向。這不僅影響了太陽能項目的規模與地點，更引發了關於「能源如何重塑衝突格局」的深層反思。
summary.en:
In a conflict where territorial disputes typically hinder renewable energy adoption, the Israeli-Palestinian conflict has transformed solar power into a strategic asset. This study reveals how Israel, the Palestinian Authority, and Hamas have weaponized solar energy to advance security priorities—often sidelining economic or environmental concerns. By decentralizing electricity generation, preventing political manipulation of shared grids, and strategically positioning solar infrastructure, solar technology has become a linchpin of national security in this volatile region.

The research identifies three key objectives driving solar deployment: mitigating grid vulnerabilities from aerial bombing in Gaza or rocket attacks against Israel; using off-grid rooftop systems in contested urban areas like East Jerusalem to avoid political disputes over shared infrastructure; and siting solar farms in West Bank territories (Areas B and C) to secure energy continuity and limit rival expansion. These applications underscore how solar energy has transcended its environmental role to become a tool of geopolitical maneuvering.

Unlike other territorial disputes, the Israeli-Palestinian conflict’s unique dynamics—intense security risks, fragmented governance, and urgent energy needs—make it particularly receptive to solar as a security tool. Factors like the need for grid resilience against military attacks, the absence of unified energy governance, and the strategic value of energy independence have driven a model where solar infrastructure serves as both shield and sword. As solar systems expand, they are reshaping not only energy landscapes but also the very nature of the conflict itself.

keyArticle.title: Solar energy as national security in the Israeli-Palestinian conflict
